author: louisabraham
text: Compilation error on aarch64

author: louisabraham
text:
## 🐛 Bug

I'm trying to compile pytorch on manjaro arm (pinebook pro).

```
pacman -Syu python python-numpy python-yaml python-cffi python-setuptools cmake

export NO_CUDA=1
export NO_DISTRIBUTED=1
export NO_MKLDNN=1 
export BUILD_TEST=0
export MAX_JOBS=6
export USE_SYSTEM_NCCL=0
export USE_NCC=OFF
export VERBOSE=1
export USE_OPENMP=1

git clone https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch --recursive
cd pytorch
python setup.py install
```

author: marty1885
text:
I ran into the same issue on aarch64. I am able to work around it by downloading sleef 3.5.1, install it to system and set the `USE_SYSTEM_SLEEF=ON` flag.

Hope it helps

author: louisabraham
text: Rad! I will definitely try tonight :)

author: marty1885
text: To be clear. I set the CMake flag (editing CMakeLists.txt), not by exporting. And I built pytorch using a modified Arch Linux's [PKGBUILD](https://github.com/archlinux/svntogit-community/tree/packages/python-pytorch/trunk). Not sure if the patches have helped in other ways.

author: louisabraham
text:

First, compile and install sleef: <https://sleef.org/compile.xhtml> (you will need sudo to install). You should also add the following line to your `.bashrc` (or equivalent)

Here is how I produced it:

author: marty1885
text:
@louis2889184 Wow, that's a very clear and easy to follow instruction!

Since you are at it. May you try if your build of PyTorch can utilize multiple cores? Mine seems to be single core only and I can't figure out why. I think I'll make another report if you are also experiencing the same issue.

author: louisabraham
text:
Thanks :)

What do you mean by "multiple cores"? Do you mean BLAS parallelism? or torch.distributed?

author: marty1885
text: Oops.. I mean BLAS parallelism.

author: louisabraham
text: What is your protocol to test?

author: marty1885
text:
Please run:

It generates a large matrix (large enough so there's enough time to observe) then performs matrix multiplication.

Then run `top`, `htop` or whatever to see if PyTorch is performing the matrix multiplication in parallel.

Mine look like this  
[![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/8792393/94040956-2efadd80-fdfc-11ea-8a01-95db285ce0eb.png)](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/8792393/94040956-2efadd80-fdfc-11ea-8a01-95db285ce0eb.png)

By the looks of the output, I may have messed up something? `at::get_num_threads()` should be 16 on my system.

**Update** I tried your .whl and it also only uses a single thread. Maybe it's a issue with my system config? IDK

author: louisabraham
text:
By default, I had blas (lapack) installed. Installing openblas made me use multiple threads (and drastically improved even single thread performance).

Also there is a strange thing going on with MKL. I don't understand why even my pytorch is built with MKL, my cmake flags are USE\_MKL : OFF but BLAS : MKL.

But yours has one more line about MKL.

Could you try:

author: louisabraham
text: Also you don't have OpenMP, which is strange as it is usually bundled with the compiler.

author: louisabraham
text: Strangely, rerunning cmake after installing OpenBLAS doesn't change the BLAS : MKL option so it's probably useless to recompile as the problem either will be solved by installing OpenBLAS or comes from another source (most likely OpenMP).

author: marty1885
text:
Ahh... so its caused by LAPACK. Installed OpenBLAS (replaces LAPACK) and it runs in parallel. Thank you!

MKL and MLK-DNN are different products (yeah, bad naming). MKL is x86 only while MKL-DNN supports a wide family of processors. The relation is kinda convoluted and I have to hack CMake to convince PyTorch MKL-DNN is available on ARM. I don't get why PyTorch prioritizes using BLAS instead of NNPACK and NKL-DNN tho.

I think I built my PyTorch with TBB instead of OpenMP. As a step debugging why nothing is running in parallel.

author: louisabraham
text:
Really nice! I don't think PyTorch can use oneDNN (they renamed it) "instead" of BLAS, they are probably not the same APIs. Maybe for more neural networky functions it will use oneDNN.

I think I built my PyTorch with TBB instead of OpenMP. As a step debugging why nothing is running in parallel.

You can probably check by testing OMP\_NUM\_THREADS and tbb::init (with ctypes) to set the number of threads manually.
